rationale: Sterling has posted two consecutive weekly gains to its highest level since mid-June, with the market absorbing UK political turmoil as the Burnham premiership transition clarifies this week — political uncertainty resolution is a near-term GBP tailwind. The pair sits exactly at its 50d/200d SMA confluence (~1.34), and with the USD under modest softening pressure and BoE rate-cut pricing remaining cautious given sticky UK services inflation, the path of least resistance is marginally higher. However conviction is low: a soft UK CPI or global risk-off shock could quickly overwhelm the modest po
key_drivers: UK political transition resolution (Burnham PM Monday)
BoE hawkish-relative-to-peers cut pace limiting GBP downside
UK CPI print binary risk mid-week
